title: Syncope neurology follow-up

examination.text: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.
examination.rationale: Supports prior events, history, examination, vital signs, ECG and selected tests for syncope follow-up; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.2.1–1.1.2.4 — history, examination, vital signs, ECG and selected tests

investigations.text: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.
investigations.rationale: Supports safety, driving and recurrence discussion for syncope follow-up; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documented discussion of the event, tests, driving, safety, and what to do if another event occurs.

follow_up.text: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.
follow_up.rationale: Supports event and witness history for syncope follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Event date, posture, prodrome, witness account, duration, recovery, injury, focal symptoms, vital signs, ECG, diagnosis and recurrence are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for syncope follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
